edges.description: IL-5-rich type 2 inflammation promotes eosinophil accumulation and activation in tissue.
Type 2 eosinophilic inflammation drives the characteristic airway-predominant phase of EGPA.
Eosinophilic cardiotoxicity contributes to occult and overt cardiac disease.
Eosinophilic neurotoxins and infiltrates contribute to axonal neuropathy.
ANCA-activated neutrophils damage endothelium and vessel walls.
ANCA-activated neutrophils and complement drive vascular wall injury and necrosis.
Vasculitic ischemia contributes to mononeuritic or axonal neuropathy.
Cutaneous small-vessel vasculitis manifests as palpable purpura.
Eosinophilic myocardial inflammation and remodeling manifest clinically as cardiomyopathy.
Combined eosinophilic and vasculitic nerve injury produces the clinical neuropathy phenotype.

nodes.description: Adult-onset asthma is a defining clinical feature and often precedes systemic vasculitis.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: EGPA occurs in patients with asthma and peripheral and tissue eosinophilia, and ~30% of the patients present antineutrophil cytoplasm antibodies (ANCA) mainly specific for myeloperoxidase (MPO)

nodes.description: Cardiac involvement is often clinically occult at diagnosis and reflects eosinophilic inflammation with chronic tissue injury and remodeling.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac Involvement in Eosinophilic Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis.
evidence.snippet: Cardiac disease is a major cause of mortality in patients with EGPA, accounting for around 50% of disease-related deaths.

nodes.description: Cardiac involvement may be clinically silent but is a major determinant of EGPA morbidity and mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: An increasing amount of evidence indicates that EGPA's clinical phenotypes tends to segregate according to ANCA-status, as the major eosinophil-driven complications are most frequently found in the ANCA-negative subset of EGPA, namely lung infiltrates, myocardiopathy, and gastrointestinal manifestations.

nodes.description: Activated eosinophils infiltrate tissues and release toxic granule proteins that injure myocardium, nerves, airways, and vascular endothelium.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: Eosinophils exhibit a wide spectrum of cytotoxicity, that is mediated by an array of enzymes stored in cytoplasmic granules, each of which associated with distinct type of clinically observable organ damage.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: Cardiac involvement is the major cause of mortality and morbidity in EGPA and has been widely associated to eosinophilia.

nodes.description: In ANCA-positive EGPA, circulating neutrophils are primed by inflammatory cytokines and C5a and then activated by MPO-ANCA, initiating the vasculitic arm of disease.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: Circulating neutrophils get primed for ANCA activation by inflammatory cytokines and C5a complement factor. Priming induces the exposition on neutrophils cell-surface of ANCA antigens.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: Activated neutrophils release factors which activate the alternative complement pathway, resulting in the generation of C5a fragment, which in turn attracts neutrophils at the site of inflammation and primes the incoming neutrophils for ANCA activation.

nodes.description: The vasculitic arm of EGPA produces ischemic and inflammatory injury across multiple organs, especially nerves, skin, and kidney.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: In contrast, MPO-ANCA-positive patients present a more “vasculitic phenotype,” which comprises palpable purpura, peripheral neuropathy, rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis and, rarely alveolar hemorrhage.

nodes.description: ANCA-activated neutrophils marginate into vessel walls and cause endothelial damage through respiratory burst, degranulation, NETosis, and necrosis.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: ANCA-activated neutrophils marginate and penetrate the vessel wall, where they undergo respiratory burst, degranulation, NETosis, and necrosis causing endothelial damage.

nodes.description: Neuropathy in EGPA reflects combined eosinophilic neurotoxicity and vasculitic injury to peripheral nerves and epineural vessels.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: The neurotoxic properties of eosinophils are clinically evident in the form of axonal neuropathy, a frequent finding in EGPA.

nodes.description: Peripheral neuropathy is a common manifestation of the vasculitic and eosinophilic nerve-injury arms of disease.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: In contrast, MPO-ANCA-positive patients present a more “vasculitic phenotype,” which comprises palpable purpura, peripheral neuropathy, rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis and, rarely alveolar hemorrhage.

nodes.description: Palpable purpura reflects cutaneous small-vessel vasculitis in the MPO-ANCA-positive phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: In contrast, MPO-ANCA-positive patients present a more “vasculitic phenotype,” which comprises palpable purpura, peripheral neuropathy, rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis and, rarely alveolar hemorrhage.

nodes.description: EGPA is driven in part by Th2-skewed immunity with IL-5-centered eosinophil expansion, tissue recruitment, and persistent eosinophilic inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis: Dissecting the Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by the en masse polarization of T helper lymphocytes toward a Th2 phenotype, the upregulation of eosinophil-selective eotaxin chemokines (particularly eotaxin-3), and an increased secretion of eosinophilotropic cytokines [i.e., interleukin (IL)-4, IL-5, IL-9, IL-13, and IL-25].
